treatment.narrative: missing/mismatched closures (normal form): [('universe', 'budget')]; loop_violation: revise re-proposed a struck declaration 'level store key store_id' — a settled mark stays settled unless the human reopens it
checklist: basis: is budget a per-store-day spine (every store has a target each day) or an events table (rows only where a target was set)? absence-as-zero vs gap depends on this.
additivity: `target` — summable across store and day? if it is a daily budget it sums; if it is ever a rate/period-normalized figure it is NON-additive. Confirm at the store-day grain.
basis: is budget a dense daily spine (every store x day) or a sparse events table? absence of a (store,day) row — zero target or gap? declare the basis.
additivity: 'target' is a budget figure — additive across store and day IF it is a per-day amount; if it is a period target replicated per row it double-counts on sum. confirm grain before summing.

refutation: store has no catalog table or PK — store_id is only an unvalidated FK-shaped column in budget. this edge points at an undeclared parent; confirm store exists as a level or degrade the edge.
basis: budget is a plan/target spine, not observed events — absence of a (store,day) row is a GAP (no plan set), not a zero; do not treat missing targets as 0.
